segments.text: Paul, a prisoner of Jesus Christ, and Timothy our brother, unto Philemon our dearly beloved, and fellowlabourer
And to our beloved Apphia, and Archippus our fellowsoldier, and to the church in thy house
Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ
segments.speaker: God and Jesus Christ

segments.text: I thank my God, making mention of thee always in my prayers
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Hearing of thy love and faith, which thou hast toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all saints
That the communication of thy faith may become effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus
For we have great joy and consolation in thy love, because the bowels of the saints are refreshed by thee, brother
Wherefore, though I might be much bold in Christ to enjoin thee that which is convenient
Yet for love's sake I rather beseech thee, being such an one as Paul the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, whom I have begotten in my bonds
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Which in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable to thee and to me
Whom I have sent again: thou therefore receive him, that is, mine own bowels
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Whom I would have retained with me, that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But without thy mind would I do nothing; that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity, but willingly
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever
Not now as a servant, but above a servant, a brother beloved, specially to me, but how much more unto thee, both in the flesh, and in the Lord
If thou count me therefore a partner, receive him as myself
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: If he hath wronged thee, or oweth thee ought, put that on mine account
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I Paul have written it with mine own hand, I will repay it: albeit I do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Yea, brother, let me have joy of thee in the Lord: refresh my bowels in the Lord
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say
But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I trust that through your prayers I shall be given unto you
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: There salute thee Epaphras, my fellowprisoner in Christ Jesus
Marcus, Aristarchus, Demas, Lucas, my fellowlabourers
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen
segments.speaker: Jesus Christ
